methodology.title: How to Read This Data
methodology.description: All indices are normalized to 2005 = 100 for comparability. This allows us to see relative change over time, but does not represent absolute values.
notes: Housing affordability index combines home prices and mortgage rates against median incomes.
CPI figures use the all-items consumer price index for Canada.
Wage data represents average hourly wages for all employees.
Education costs include tuition only; total cost of attendance is higher.
Regional data may use different methodologies; compare with caution.

items.detail: Generational boundaries vary by source. We use commonly accepted ranges but acknowledge overlap and variation.
National averages mask significant regional variation. Urban/rural and provincial differences are substantial.
Survey methodologies change over time. Long-term trends should be interpreted with caution.
Wealth (accumulated assets) and income (annual earnings) tell different stories. Both matter.
Real vs nominal values can significantly change interpretation. We note when values are inflation-adjusted.
